hypothesis: Incorporating the total system charge as an explicit input and jointly training equivariant GNN MLIP models to predict per-atom partial charges as an auxiliary task will reduce force MAE on charged/ionic molecular test sets by at least 15% and increase the fraction of stable 1 ns NVE MD trajectories (defined as total energy conserved within ±1 kcal/mol per atom) by at least 25% compared to identical models trained without charge information.
